title: SMIC Begins Testing Domestic Immersion DUV Lithography Tool
gist: SMIC has begun testing a domestically-built immersion DUV lithography tool from Shanghai startup Yuliangsheng (backed by former Huawei engineers), while sister company Si Carrier pursues EUV under the internal codename 'Mount Everest' — but experts say high-volume domestic DUV production is still at least two years away.

insights.claim: SMIC has begun testing a domestically manufactured immersion DUV lithography machine built by Yuliangsheng, a government-backed Shanghai startup staffed with many former Huawei engineers, according to the Financial Times.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: First concrete signal of a domestic immersion DUV tool reaching test phase at China's leading foundry — a milestone for tracking China's equipment self-sufficiency progress against US export controls.
insights.quote: China’s leading foundry, SMIC (Semiconductor Manufacturing International Corp.), has begun testing a domestically manufactured immersion DUV lithography machine.

insights.claim: Industry experts estimate it will take at least two years before this domestic DUV lithography tool can be deployed for high-volume integrated circuit production.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: Sets a concrete timeline (2028+) for when domestic DUV could meaningfully displace ASML tools in Chinese fabs — a milestone investors in ASML, Applied Materials, and Chinese foundries should track.
insights.quote: Industry experts estimate that it will take at least two years before this domestic DUV lithography machine can be deployed for high-volume integrated circuit production.

insights.claim: Yuliangsheng is a subsidiary of Si Carrier (Shenzhen), which is separately developing EUV lithography equipment under the internal codename 'Mount Everest.'
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: Signals a coordinated, parallel-track Chinese push on both DUV and EUV equipment via a single corporate group — a name (Si Carrier / Mount Everest) worth tracking for future EUV-substitute headlines.
insights.quote: under an ambitious project internally codenamed “Mount Everest.”

insights.claim: Shanghai Micro Electronics Equipment (SMEE) is developing a 193nm ArF immersion DUV system capable of 28nm manufacturing, and with advanced multi-patterning could potentially reach 7nm, albeit with complexity and yield trade-offs.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: Suggests China has more than one credible domestic DUV lithography path (SMEE plus Yuliangsheng), raising the odds of eventual import substitution even if node performance lags TSMC/Samsung.
insights.quote: SMEE’s tool could potentially support processes down to 7nm, though with complexity and yield trade-offs.

insights.claim: Chinese fabs including SMIC continue to rely on previously purchased ASML DUV machines, since US export restrictions now ban further sales of even DUV equipment to China.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: Confirms the installed base of ASML DUV tools in China is now a fixed, non-replenishable asset — reinforces the strategic urgency behind China's domestic equipment push and the ceiling on ASML's China revenue growth.
insights.quote: Chinese fabs such as SMIC continue to rely on ASML’s DUV lithography machines, which were purchased before U.S. export restrictions banned further sales to China.
